title: Aurelius, Meditations §aur_08_xxxiii: As almost all her other faculties and properties the nature of the universe h...
body: As almost all her other faculties and properties the nature of the universe hath imparted unto every reasonable creature, so this in particular we have received from her, that as whatsoever doth oppose itself unto her, and doth withstand her in her purposes and intentions, she doth, though against its will and intention, bring it about to herself, to serve herself of it in the execution of her own destinated ends; and so by this though not intended co-operation of it with herself makes it part of herself whether it will or no. So may every reasonable creature, what crosses and impediments soever it meets with in the course of this mortal life, it may use them as fit and proper objects, to the furtherance of whatsoever it intended and absolutely proposed unto itself as its natural end and happiness.
author: engine

witnesses.label: Vaticanus Graecus 1950 — earliest complete Greek MS
Dalfen, Marci Aurelii Antonini Ad Se Ipsum Libri XII (Teubner 1987)
George Long translation (1862) — public domain English
